programme.desc: A series of murders are linked to the tragic deaths of schoolchildren 50 years earlier.

programme.title: Naach Lucky Naach

programme.title: Kaho Naa Pyaar Hai
programme.desc: A poor singer Rohit and rich Sonia fall in love, are stranded on a deserted island, rise then Rohit dies; Sonia finds a Rohit-lookalike, Raj Chopra.

programme.title: Deva
programme.desc: Dev Ambre, a ruthless cop, loses his memory after solving a murder. He must reinvestigate, hidden from everyone except DCP Farhan Khan during dangerous investigations.

programme.title: RRR
programme.desc: A fearless revolutionary and an officer in the British force, who once shared a deep bond, decide to join forces and chart out an inspirational path of freedom against the despotic rulers.

programme.title: Race
programme.desc: Two stepbrothers own a huge stud farm and a horse racing business. When they learn that they have a common love interest, one of them tries to kill the other and inherit the insurance money.

programme.title: Vicky Donor
programme.desc: Man asked to donate a lot and falls for anti donor girl.

programme.title: Dunki
programme.desc: Friends embark on a treacherous 'donkey flight' to the UK, risking their lives via a clandestine route in pursuit of a better future.

programme.desc: Gunjan Saxena eyes becoming a pilot after being drawn by the idea of a life in a cockpit. She fulfils her dream and serves India in the Kargil War.

programme.title: 1920 London

programme.title: Tamasha

programme.desc: Man saves money for father's Char Dham Yatra dream, tragedy strikes in floods.

programme.title: Jayasimha

programme.title: Simmba

programme.title: Himmatwar
programme.desc: A set of circumstances makes Vasu, a moneylender, save the life of a policeman, Sivaraman Nayak. But that only leads to Vasu appearing on the radar of a gangster and contract killer.

programme.title: Spyder

programme.title: Raangi

programme.title: Mr. & Mrs. Mahi
programme.desc: A former cricketer turned coach, played by Rajkummar Rao, helps his wife, Janhvi Kapoor, achieve stardom in the sport, but struggles to cope with her rising fame and his own fading glory.

programme.title: Kisko Tha Pata

programme.title: Sandwich
programme.desc: Shekar has fallen in love with Nisha, but his sister is set to marry Harpreet, but on the condition that Shekar marry Harpreet's sister. Unable to refuse, Shekar must lead a double life

programme.title: Ajay
programme.desc: Manorama and Ajay fall in love with each other despite the differences in their class and upbringing. However, her malicious uncles refuse to accept their relationship and try to get rid of Ajay.

programme.title: Koyla
programme.desc: Shah Rukh Khan plays a mute servant betrayed by his love, left for dead, who returns to seek revenge on those who wronged him, leading to a journey filled with pain and retribution.

programme.title: Sabse Bada Khiladi
programme.desc: A police officer finds evidence that an influential criminal lawyer of the city had plotted his brother's murder twenty years ago. He spins an intricate web of lies to trap the lawyer in his own game.

programme.title: Raja Ki Aayegi Baraat
programme.desc: Mala is raped by Raja; a judge orders marriage to her perpetrator as punishment. Despite cruelty, Mala remains kind and eventually wins them over.

programme.title: Kudrat Ka Kanoon
programme.desc: As soon as Vijay is released from prison, he goes in search of his wife and child. He finds out that his wife was imprisoned for the murder of their child and is now living in a brothel.

programme.title: An Art Lovers' Guide
programme.desc: Janina Ramirez and Alastair Sooke discover Barcelona through its art and architecture, including the frescoes that inspired Miro and a surprising collection of vintage fans.

programme.title: Lost Cities of the Ancients
programme.desc: This episode looks at the legendary lost city of Piramesse. This magnificent ancient capital was built 3,000 years ago by the Egyptian pharaoh Ramesses the Great, but long ago the whole city disappeared. When it was rediscovered by early archaeologists, it opened up a bizarre puzzle - when Piramesse was finally found it was in the wrong place, somewhere Ramesses the Great could not possibly have built it. Recreating the stories of both the early archaeologists and the ancient Egyptians, the film enters a lost world, recounting the strange tale of the quest for Piramesse and following the intriguing detective work of modern archaeologists Manfred Bietak and Edgar Pusch as they solve the baffling mystery of how this great lost city could vanish, only to reappear thousands of years later in the wrong place.

programme.title: The Yorkshire Dales
programme.desc: Paul Rose’s Yorkshire Dales adventure takes him to Wharfedale. He meets the butchers in Ilkley whose Yorkshire produce is being sold all over the country. Paul also heads for Bolton Abbey and spends time with the Duke of Devonshire. He then takes time out with the Calendar Girls, who shot to fame after their nude calendar became a worldwide sensation two decades ago, and has a close encounter with bats that live along the River Wharfe. Finally, he meets the families from inner-city Bradford who are having their first Dales experience.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The real side of boat life with Robbie Cumming. Robbie navigates the mighty River Severn and takes an unexpected bath as he takes a tumble at the Tardebigge lock flight in Worcestershire.

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: The Mandrin Cave in southern France has been attracting the attention of archaeologists for 30 years. The cave was regularly occupied in prehistoric times, and a remarkable record of human habitation going back thousands of years has been preserved by its soils. In 2015, the remains of a Neanderthal were uncovered, and the archaeological evidence points to this individual, named Thorin, living in close proximity to newly arrived Homo sapiens. This documentary follows an international team of scientists as they discover and investigate precious relics of the Palaeolithic period, resulting in groundbreaking revelations about the human populations of around 50,000 years ago as researchers uncover some of the secrets of the Mandrin Cave.

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary which follows the construction of a trailblazing 36,000-tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 Chernobyl disaster. It films close up with the team of international engineers as they race to build the new structure before Chernobyl's original concrete sarcophagus - the hastily built structure that covers the reactor - collapses. Built to last just 30 years, the temporary sarcophagus is now crumbling, putting the world at risk of another release of radioactive dust. Radiation levels make it impossible for workers to build the new shelter directly over the old reactor, so engineers are erecting the new megastructure - taller than the tower of Big Ben and three times heavier than the Eiffel Tower - to one side and will then face the challenge of sliding the largest object ever moved on land into place over the old reactor.

programme.title: Play for Today
programme.desc: Patricia Hayes stars in Jeremy Sandford’s play about a down and out woman who wanders through life in an alcoholic haze without a job, a home or any money.

programme.title: How to Get Ahead
programme.desc: Writer, broadcaster and Newsnight arts correspondent Stephen Smith looks back at the medieval age to find out what it took to get ahead at the court of Richard II. Richard presided over the first truly sophisticated and artistic court in England. Painters, sculptors, poets, tailors, weavers and builders flocked to court to make their fortunes. But these were dangerous times. Being close to Richard brought many a courtier to a sticky end. Featuring David Tennant and Clarissa Dickson Wright.

programme.title: The Lost Neanderthals
programme.desc: The Mandrin Cave in southern France has been attracting the attention of archaeologists for 30 years. The cave was regularly occupied in prehistoric times, and a remarkable record of human habitation going back thousands of years has been preserved by its soils. In 2015, the remains of a Neanderthal were uncovered, and the archaeological evidence points to this individual, named Thorin, living in close proximity to newly arrived Homo sapiens. This documentary follows an international team of scientists as they discover and investigate precious relics of the Palaeolithic period, resulting in groundbreaking revelations about the human populations of around 50,000 years ago as researchers uncover some of the secrets of the Mandrin Cave.

programme.title: Timewatch
programme.desc: First transmitted in 2001, this programme chronicles the construction of the Empire State Building in New York, which was the world's tallest skyscraper when opened in 1931. The programme investigates the building's history through interviews with the people who contributed to the construction of this iconic building.

programme.title: Inside Chernobyl's Mega Tomb
programme.desc: Documentary which follows the construction of a trailblazing 36,000-tonne steel structure to entomb the ruins of the nuclear power plant destroyed in the 1986 Chernobyl disaster. It films close up with the team of international engineers as they race to build the new structure before Chernobyl's original concrete sarcophagus - the hastily built structure that covers the reactor - collapses. Built to last just 30 years, the temporary sarcophagus is now crumbling, putting the world at risk of another release of radioactive dust. Radiation levels make it impossible for workers to build the new shelter directly over the old reactor, so engineers are erecting the new megastructure - taller than the tower of Big Ben and three times heavier than the Eiffel Tower - to one side and will then face the challenge of sliding the largest object ever moved on land into place over the old reactor.

programme.title: Pubs, Ponds and Power: The Story of the Village
programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson unlocks the ancient roots of the Northumberland village of Warkworth. With the help of locals, he discovers clues that point back almost 1,000 years to the Norman conquest when the invaders laid the foundations of a planned community, still visible to this day.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: The reality of life afloat with Robbie Cumming. On the last leg of his journey across England, Robbie crosses an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon and gets stuck in a lock in central Birmingham.

programme.title: Murder on the Orient Express
programme.desc: Returning to England from Istanbul on the Orient Express, Hercule Poirot is asked to investigate a murder.

programme.title: Talking Pictures
programme.desc: A retrospective look at television appearances made over the years by award-winning actor Albert Finney, with interviews from the archive and classic clips capturing the milestones and highlights of his life and career. Narrated by Sylvia Syms.

programme.title: The Importance of Being Earnest
programme.desc: Screen adaptation of Oscar Wilde's celebrated play in which two Victorian gentlemen both adopt a double identity under the name of Ernest and end up in trouble when they both turn up at the same country house one weekend.

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Sun
programme.desc: Ninety-million miles away from us is the power that shapes our world - the sun. We see it shine in the sky above us, but beyond our sight, something dramatic is happening - the sun is going into overdrive. It's more active now than it's been for a decade, sending eruptions of super-heated plasma and vast waves of radiation towards our planet. With the potential to disrupt our lives in dramatic ways. Using the latest satellite images, and the expertise of Britain's leading solar scientists, Kate Humble and Helen Czerski reveal the inner workings of our very own star, and the influence its mysterious cycles of activity have on our planet. They discover why the light reaching us from the sun can be up to a million years old; they meet the teams who protect us by keeping a round-the-clock vigil on the sun; and investigate why some scientists think longer term changes in the sun's behaviour may have powerful effects on our climate.

programme.title: The Riviera: A History in Pictures
programme.desc: Richard E Grant explores how modern art and the Riviera grew up together when France's Cote d'Azur became the hedonistic playground and experimental studio for the great masters of 20th-century painting. With Henri Matisse and Pablo Picasso resident on the coast, other artists from Jean Cocteau to Henri Lartigue, Raoul Dufy to Fernand Leger and Francis Picabia to Sergei Diaghilev were drawn to the area. As transatlantic liners brought America's super-rich to the region, art and celebrity became integrally intertwined as cultural gurus and multimillionaires all partied on the beach. In an era of sunshine and bathing, of cinema and fast cars, of the Ballets Russes and Monte Carlo casinos, Grant discovers the extraordinary output of what became briefly the world's creative hub.

programme.title: The Jet Stream and Us
programme.desc: Documentary tracing how human understanding of the jet stream - a ribbon of fast moving air high in the atmosphere - has grown. It has been responsible for bewildering effect on bomber pilots during the Second World War, turbocharging modern transatlantic flyers, the infamous 1987 hurricane and devastating floods. Scientists believe this powerful weather phenomenon is now changing its pattern of behaviour and could have an even bigger impact on our climate and the way we live our lives. Interviewees include Sir Brian Hoskins, University of Reading and Kirsty McCabe from the BBC Weather Centre.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 24 September 1999 and featuring Vengaboys, Sting, Everything but the Girl, Paul Johnson, Wyclef Jean & Bono, David Bowie, Tom Jones & The Cardigans and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Jayne Middlemiss presents a roadshow edition of the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 1 October 1999 and featuring DJ Jean, Shania Twain, Kelle Bryan, David Bowie, S Club 7, Britney Spears, Lou Bega and Eiffel 65.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Gary Davies and Nicky Campbell present the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 28 April 1988 and featuring Pat and Mick, James Brown, Fairground Attraction, Brenda Russell, The Primitives, Luther Vandross, Joyce Sims, Bananarama, Will Downing, S'Express and Scott Fitzgerald.

programme.title: Top of the Pops
programme.desc: Nicky Campbell presents the pop chart programme, first broadcast on 25 April 1991 and featuring EMF, Vic Reeves and Electronic.

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: Monty is busy this week as the garden bursts into colour. Woody herbs such as thyme and sage don’t thrive in his clay soil, so he’s sowing seeds to ensure a steady supply. He’s planting grasses in the new Woodland Garden and creating a colourful pot for shade, and there's advice on what to do with this year’s daffodil bulbs. Carol Klein proves that small can be both beautiful and delicious, creating a modest border devoted to vegetables and weaving it together with flowers to provide year round colour. And Advolly Richmond heads to Fife to uncover the story of the first pink cupped daffodil, bred in the early 1900s and still growing today thanks to two passionate plantswomen. There’s a visit to a specialist nursery in Somerset. With help from the Royal Botanic Gardens, Kew, they are preparing to exhibit trees fit for a changing climate at the RHS Chelsea Flower show. And it's all aboard a houseboat that's jam-packed with plants.

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A second chance to explore the BBC’s archives with one of British music’s biggest and best-loved stars, Dusty Springfield. The great soul diva and her haunting voice are captured once again in selection of BBC performances from over the years, including You Don’t Have to Say You Love Me, Son of a Preacher Man, and Bacharach and David’s Look of Love. Together, these songs underline why Dusty earned her reputation as one of pop’s great talents, who deserves her ranking alongside fellow singing legends like Aretha Franklin and Dionne Warwick.

programme.title: Dusty Springfield at the BBC
programme.desc: A selection of Dusty Springfield's performances at the BBC from 1961 to 1995. Dusty was one of Britain's great pop divas, guaranteed to give us a big melody in songs soaring with drama and yearning. The clips show Dusty's versatility as an artist and performer and include songs from her folk beginnings with The Springfields; the melodrama of You Don't Have to Say You Love Me; Dusty's homage to Motown with Heatwave and Nowhere to Run; the Jacques Brel song If You Go Away; the Bacharach and David tune The Look of Love; and Dusty's collaboration with Pet Shop Boys in the late 1980s. There are also some great duets from Dusty's career with Tom Jones and Mel Torme.

programme.title: Sad Songs at the BBC
programme.desc: A journey through a selection of some of the saddest but finest songs from the BBC’s music archives. Amongst the artists performing are Paul McCartney, Kate Bush, The Smiths, Randy Crawford, Coldplay, Dolly Parton and Eric Clapton, as well as Eric Carmen singing his anthem for the lonely, All By Myself.

programme.title: Tech Now
programme.desc: Alasdair Keane visits the underground insect farm turning food waste into animal feed. Shiona McCallum takes a tour behind the scenes of Blenheim Palace to see how new technologies are playing a key role in the restoration of the Unesco World Heritage. Yasmin Morgan-Griffiths explores how research linking climate change and extreme weather events is being used in lawsuits against major polluters. And Martin Sharkey takes a journey into the history of the emoji.

programme.title: AI Decoded
programme.desc: The AI revolution demands vast amounts of electricity. New data centres powering the next generation of artificial intelligence can use as much energy as a small city. Last year, the world's biggest tech firms spent more than 400 billion dollars expanding this infrastructure and demand is still growing. But there's a problem. In the United States, four in ten data centre projects planned for this year face serious delays, and increasingly the public are raising concerns about living next door to these colossal digital factories.

programme.desc: Alasdair Keane visits the underground insect farm turning food waste into animal feed. Shiona McCallum takes a tour behind the scenes of Blenheim Palace to see how new technologies are playing a key role in the restoration of the Unesco World Heritage. Yasmin Morgan-Griffiths explores how research linking climate change and extreme weather events is being used in lawsuits against major polluters. And Martin Sharkey takes a journey into the history of the emoji.

programme.title: Breakfast

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Gloria Hunniford, Julia Somerville and Louise Minchin investigate the home insurance policies refusing damage claims if the cause can be linked to events that were insured under an old policy – and they reveal the insurers taking differing views on when to pay out. Also on the programme, an investigation into the mobility furniture company letting down some vulnerable elderly customers as well as TV presenter Pam Rhodes. And at the advice clinic in South Shields, experts hear from one couple about a faulty sofa that the manufacturer won’t replace.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Hampshire flat Jacqui views gets the boutique hotel look. Martin is in Gloucestershire, where the layout of a mid-terrace leaves a lot to be desired. He also visits Hertfordshire, where a contentious landlord is looking to go the extra mile at another flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Eric Knowles and the teams are in Hertfordshire with experts Colin Young and Raj Bisram. Each team is hoping to make big profits, but will they win golden gavels at the auction in Norfolk? Eric also learns about the remarkable story of the Women’s Land Army as he meets a woman with an amazing collection of memorabilia.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Abergavenny, couple Claudia and Luke clash over instincts and risk everything in a rain-soaked race, ending in a last-minute upcycle scramble and a tense live auction.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo’s on home turf as he’s called in to redesign part of the Western Isles Hotel in Tobermory on the Isle of Mull. New owner Brian is entrusting him with a large salon room, which works as a spa and massage room by day and an intimate bar by night. With two separate clients to please in one room, Banjo has his work cut out. He decides to go for a complete, bold transformation, but finds himself completely out of his comfort zone in trying to hit both briefs.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Denise Nurse helps a couple from a busy town in Buckinghamshire, who are about to become empty-nesters, find a new home in beautiful Northumberland. However, this move isn’t just about them, as grandma is coming too. Denise lines up a string of homes to tempt her buyers with far-reaching views, including a mystery house she hopes will provide the perfect package to suit the whole family. Denise also heads to one of the county’s beautiful beaches, where she meets a retired GP whose new passion is creating extraordinary artworks in the sand.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Furniture maker Matt from Norfolk and textile designer Jess from Somerset are stepping into Dom’s makers’ bootcamp. Ex-musician Matt uses traditional methods and hand tools to create beautiful furniture but has a confidence problem. Mentor Bruce Kenneth needs to find a way of getting Matt to believe in his work and speed up production. It’s a big job, but Bruce is a man on a mission. Textile designer Jess found her passion for fabrics whilst experiencing postnatal anxiety. She has bags of talent, but she’s focused on making cushions. Will mentor Piyush Suri be able to help Jess broaden her product range and start making the money her skills deserve?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, family members Sharan, Dips, Vik and Preeti – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Reform UK
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by Reform UK for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Vernon Kay bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Phil’s search continues, two Walford residents face a race against time, and Nigel receives some visitors.

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: It’s a family affair as Steve Fletcher fixes a model watermill while son Fred brings a cherished clock back to life. The team also fix a stunning Notting Hill Carnival headpiece.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: A fresh set of hopeful home cooks serve up their signature dishes and precision cooking in a bid to impress the MasterChef judges and get through to the quarter-finals.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: UEFA Champions League
programme.desc: Arsenal travel to Atlético Madrid in the first leg of the Champions League semi-finals, with the Gunners looking to reach the final for the first time in 20 years. Holders Paris Saint-Germain take on six-time winners Bayern Munich, with striker Harry Kane having scored 12 goals in the competition already this season.

programme.title: Denmark
programme.desc: Divorced, demoralised and nearly destitute in Wales, Herb hits upon the dodgy idea of heading to Denmark, intent on being thrown in a Danish jail – which seems more comfy and safe than life at home.

programme.title: Weather for the Week Ahead
programme.desc: Detailed weather forecast.

programme.title: Joins BBC News
programme.desc: BBC One joins the BBC's rolling news channel for a night of news.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: In a special scams episode, the team meets the 20-year-old who was waiting to receive a tax rebate from HMRC, only to discover someone else had cashed the cheque weeks earlier. A fraud lawyer is on hand to explain what went wrong. Also on the programme, the advice clinic visits Reading to hear from one man who used social media to complain about missing Christmas presents he had ordered – and was unknowingly targeted by scammers. And the latest how-to guide reveals exactly what to do if your phone gets stolen.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Dion is in Derby to see a plot of land, with a whole family getting involved to build a brand new house. A separate family project in Lowestoft, Suffolk, sees an auntie and nephew tackle a tired terrace. And in another Suffolk property, Jacqui meets a novice renovator more used to dealing with cuts of meat than knocking down walls.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Since the Hollywood move was released, vintage Barbie dolls have shot up in value. Natasha Raskin Sharp meets collector Dawn Austin, whose collection of more than 800 dolls takes up an entire room of her house! Natasha learns that some vintage models are worth thousands, but does a first edition Ken doll command the same price as an early Barbie? In the Derbyshire town of Heanor, experts Mark Stacey and Catherine Southon lead the reds and blues around a huge antiques centre. Mark guides couple Terry and Alison, who plan to ‘spend little and win big’, while Catherine’s team, Craig and Dave, are looking for ‘things that are big, bold and beautiful’. During their shop, Dave spots a bingo ball wheel – but would they hit the jackpot if they took it to auction? Meanwhile, Terry has his eye on a giant eagle, but Mark’s not sure the carved bird will fly with the bidders at Colin Young’s saleroom in Grantham.

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Shift the Thrift
programme.desc: In Eastbourne, siblings David and Theresa battle to raise money for DKMS, gambling on designer fashion, vintage finds and bold upcycled pieces.

programme.title: Designing the Hebrides
programme.desc: Banjo and his team are on South Uist in the Outer Hebrides. They’ve been called in to help crofters DJ and Lindsay, whose family have lived and worked the land there for generations. The couple want to turn their garage into a shop to sell their produce from. The dull breezeblock building has no character or charm, so Banjo has to work from scratch to transform this empty space. He searches for salvage around the island and from DJ’s crofter mum, and he decides to clad the walls in disused corrugated iron panels he finds around the island. But with a budget of only £4,000, will he pull off his homestead kitchen design vision?

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A young couple want to swap their London flat for something bigger in the East Midlands, where Nicki Chapman opens the front doors to a lovely selection of homes with charming gardens. Nicki can’t miss the opportunity to meet another young couple who’ve already made their escape to develop a luxury leather goods business. After all, Nicki does love a good bag!

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Pyrographer Chantal from Leeds and stained-glass artist Claire from Norfolk both hope to make their dreams of a creative career come true. Having spent several years trying to earn a living from pyrography, Chantal feels it’s her last chance to make her craft pay. She has a thyroid condition which causes extreme fatigue, but she’s prepared to do whatever it takes to make her business dream a reality. After having to give up her sugar craft shop following a 33-year career, Claire needs to find a new way of earning a living and has her sights set on a new stained-glass business to pay the bills. Mentor Derek thinks she has the talent and potential to do well, but can Claire learn the new skills needed to succeed? Both are given two months to take on board all the advice they’re given and turn their passions into paying professions.

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents the quiz show where a team of four – in this episode, family members Conn, Paula, Brian and Sophia – take on the Bridge of Lies. One by one, they all face the Bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each crossing has 32 potential steps, including 22 truths and ten lies. They must cross the Bridge by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Step on a lie and the Bridge will halve their money. Step on three lies, and they are out of the game. There is always a safe path across the Bridge. They just have to find it. Once they have each faced the Bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Albert Square comes together to celebrate a friend, Phil battles his inner demons, and Max and Cindy’s romance causes friction.

programme.title: Race Across the World
programme.desc: At the halfway stage of the race, the teams discover their next checkpoint lies within Zaamin National Park, Uzbekistan. To reach it, they must navigate the endless horizons of the vast Kazakh steppe.

programme.title: MasterChef
programme.desc: The second quarter-final is here, and the six best cooks of the week return to prove they have more culinary skill in the tank, starting with an invention test.

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: The latest news, sport, business and weather from the BBC's Breakfast team.

programme.title: Morning Live
programme.desc: Join Gethin Jones, Helen Skelton, Michelle Ackerley and the team for conversation, advice and tips to make your day that little bit easier.

programme.title: Rip Off Britain
programme.desc: Can you trust airport departure boards? The team meet one group of passengers who waited patiently in the departure lounge for their delayed flight, only to find out it had already left without them. Also on the programme, an investigation into one of the biggest names in travel finds it’s not following the rules designed to protect people whose package holidays go wrong. And the advice clinic team head to Mansfield to help one woman who has spent more than £10,000 trying to exit her timeshare agreement and is now fielding calls from scammers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A tale of two very different million-pound-plus projects, one in central London, the other in Anglesey. Martin’s also in Bristol to see a two-bedroom flat.

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: There’s no time for horsing around as two teams canter through an antiques fair at Wetherby racecourse in West Yorkshire. In red, and saddling up for some fun, are sisters Sharon and Hayley, with their expert Charles Hanson. On the blue team, brothers Harley and Joe are paired with expert Philip Serrell. With one hour on the clock, the reds close in on an item that’s on their shopping list – an enamelled scent bottle – but they turn their noses up when they learn the best price. Over with the blues, Phil tries to spice things up with a silver nutmeg grater. Sadly, the three-figure price tag puts them off, so they stop to consider another important question – which sauce they prefer on their chips! Meanwhile, Danny Sebastian is dishing up a challenge for fairgoers, asking them to identify a mystery metal object. It’s silver-plated with a plunger at one end and a perforated acorn-shaped bowl at the other. What could it be?

programme.title: BBC News at One
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Rock 'n' roll reaches Kembleford when a group of bikers cause outrage and excitement in equal measure.

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Steve Brown is on hand to help a couple of newlyweds looking to escape South London and set down roots in the stunning countryside of Cumbria. They’ve already discounted a dozen properties, so the pressure is on for Steve to deliver somewhere that will become their very first home together.

programme.title: Make It at Market
programme.desc: Blacksmith Clare from Solihull and baker Gavin from Bedfordshire both dream of building businesses from their passions. After deciding teaching wasn’t for her, Clare turned to metalwork and retrained as a blacksmith. Having just finished her studies, she needs to start making money so that she and her partner can save up a deposit for their first home together. Orchestra box office manager Gavin hopes that baking will also help him save enough money to move house. He currently lives with his mum, and although she is the inspiration behind his baking, he wants his own place with more space for him and his equipment. Both have talent, and their mentors believe they could be booking the removal vans in no time, but will Clare and Gavin put in the hard graft to be successful?

programme.title: Bridge of Lies
programme.desc: Ross Kemp presents a celebrity edition of the quiz show, where a team of soap stars, featuring Coronation Street’s Faye Brookes, Hollyoaks’s Richard Blackwood, Natalie Anderson from Emmerdale and EastEnders’ John Partridge, get together to take on the Bridge of Lies for their chosen charities. One by one they all face the bridge, choosing who faces each crossing depending on the question category. Each bridge crossing has 32 potential steps - 22 truths and 10 lies - and they must cross by stepping on the truths and avoiding the lies, earning money for each correct answer. Once they have each faced the bridge on their own, those who have been successful will join forces to face the final crossing for a chance to take home the cash.

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong is joined by co-host Angela Scanlon for the brainy quiz where the contestants try to score as few points as possible by plumbing the depths of their general knowledge to come up with the answers no-one else could think of.

programme.title: BBC News at Six
programme.desc: The latest national and international news from the BBC.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: Party Election Broadcasts: Liberal Democrats
programme.desc: A party election broadcast by the Liberal Democrats for the local and mayoral elections on 7 May.

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Clara Amfo bring you the stories that matter from across the country.

programme.title: Extraordinary Portraits
programme.desc: A rare medical condition meant Grace was unable to give birth, as she was born without a womb. But in a landmark moment for British medical history, her sister Amy made an extraordinary decision: to donate her womb to her sister in a complicated and involved operation, which lasted hours. Just months after the transplant, Grace became pregnant and now, with her husband, has a healthy daughter. It was a gift that transformed not only the sisters’ lives but those of everyone around them in a story of love, sacrifice and a deep family bond. Bill Bailey pairs Grace and Amy with Karen Turner, an award-winning artist with a powerful commitment to telling women’s stories. Karen works using an unusual and unforgiving technique, painting directly onto raw canvas. With no background layer and no possibility of correction, every paint stroke must be right. As Karen begins her work, she faces a profound challenge: how to capture the depth of love, trust and sacrifice that made this miracle possible. Can she find a way to express not just the medical achievement but the emotional truth behind it and create a portrait worthy of this remarkable act of sisterhood?

programme.title: Beyond Paradise
programme.desc: It’s Humphrey’s last day as detective inspector, but there’s no time to dwell, as the whole team is eagerly awaiting the town’s annual pirate festival. Danger, however, is ahoy when Geraldine Desai’s nautical exhibition goes up in smoke, leading to the ancient treasure map of pirate Elias Cutler going missing amid the chaos. On the trail of the missing map, Humphrey soon has suspicions when he discovers that Geraldine has been selling her memorabilia to pay for her husband’s care and recently had the map revalued. Touching base with an antiques dealer, who is adamant the map is worth nothing, the team begin to question if they’re dealing with a harmless prank, an insurance job, or something more sinister. Meanwhile, Humphrey frets about a potential leaving party as Mr Smith prepares to take over. Smelling a rat, Anne recruits Martha and Kelby to launch an investigation of her own. Elsewhere, Esther braces herself to say goodbye to her daughter, unaware that Zoe has prepared a shock surprise.

programme.title: Have I Got News for You
programme.desc: Richard Ayoade hosts, with guest panellists actor and comedian Stephen Mangan and journalist Ava Santina Evans joining team captains Paul Merton and Ian Hislop as they delve into the news.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Mairéad and the lads take the family car on a road trip to pick up her unpredictable mother, but disaster strikes.

programme.title: BBC News at Ten
programme.desc: The latest national and international news, with reports from BBC correspondents worldwide.

programme.title: BBC London
programme.desc: The latest news, sport and weather from London.

programme.title: The Young Offenders
programme.desc: Sun and sand are the new dream, but how can the lads afford it? Enter Billy Murphy with a daring whiskey heist idea.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Joyce suddenly needs to redecorate the new maternity unit, Alex is back from holiday in Hawaii, and Serena doesn’t take it well when she finds out Matt asked to work separately from her.

programme.title: St Denis Medical
programme.desc: Bruce is attacked in the car park. Joyce can't find the money needed to improve security, so Alex takes matters into her own hands. Ron doesn’t believe Serena could beat him in a fight.

programme.title: Quiz Lady
programme.desc: A game show-obsessed young woman and her estranged train wreck of a sister must work together to help cover their mother’s gambling debts. Together, they set out on a wild, cross-country trek to get the cash the only way they know – by becoming bona fide game show champions.

programme.title: The Beauty Queen and the Catfish
programme.desc: Dr David Graham is online and looking for love. He seems to have it all: the looks, the job, and the charisma. On paper, he’s Kilmarnock’s most eligible bachelor. But when the town’s former beauty queen Abbie Draper starts to receive messages from him after he treats her grandfather in hospital, she gets the feeling he might be too good to be true. She turns online sleuth and starts to uncover a trail of deception, crazy coincidences and stories that just don’t add up. Dr David isn’t just a bad boyfriend; something more sinister is afoot. Abbie starts trying to figure out who David Graham really is and why he is doing this.

programme.title: Louis Theroux
programme.desc: For hundreds of years, romantic relationships in western society have been based on the idea of monogamy: two people, together, for ever. But with nearly half of all US marriages ending in divorce, perhaps the traditional model is no longer fit for purpose. In Love Without Limits, Louis travels to Portland, Oregon, the heart of a movement seeking to rewrite the rulebook on how we conduct intimate relationships and experience family life. Portland, whose city motto is 'Keep Portland Weird', is seen as the US capital of polyamory and ethical non-monogamy, the practice of openly and transparently having multiple relationships that go further than just sex. Louis embeds himself with families who have made the bold decision to open up their relationships in a number of different ways, whether inviting others into existing relationships or allowing partners to seek romance elsewhere. He discovers that for many, more partners means more love and more happiness. But for others, multiple relationships have led to jealousy, upset and broken hearts.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Dark States
programme.desc: Milwaukee is said to be one of the most racially divided and impoverished cities in the US. With spiralling gun crime and homicide rates, this Midwestern city encapsulates America's complex and troubled relationship with guns and the increasing disharmony between African-American communities and the police. Louis spends time with the Milwaukee Police Department as they patrol District 5, home to some of the nation's deadliest streets, with a homicide rate over twelve times the national average, and follows the homicide division as they investigate one of the city's many killings. He also embeds with a family who have recently lost a loved one to gun crime, and he meets a local social activist, once a criminal and gang leader herself, who has turned her life around and is coming up with her own solution to the blight of gun crime. On the streets of Milwaukee, Louis discovers a community who is often misunderstood by, and mistrustful of, the police. Arriving at a time of heightened tension between the police and the African-American community due to a recent police shooting, Louis hears from both sides of the debate and uncovers hope in a desperate city.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: Steve and the crew visit the southern states of the USA in search of more predators to add to his Deadly list. He dives into the crystal clear waters of the Silver Springs river to swim with a wild alligator and adds his first plant to the list, a carnivorous venus flytrap. He also feels the sting of the formidable fire ant and joins a team of scientists tracking and tagging wild bobcats.

programme.title: Deadly 60
programme.desc: In this Deadly 60 special, Steve Backshall takes a look at some of the super skills and senses that animals around the world use to hunt and survive. These range from the blistering speed and acceleration of the cheetah to the elastic tongue and 360-degree vision of the chameleon and even a shark with a remarkable hidden sixth sense.

programme.title: Interior Design Masters with Alan Carr
programme.desc: The nine designers storm a medieval-style castle to turn hotel bedrooms into perfect love nests as they draw on their own ideas of what makes a romantic retreat for inspiration. They are able to reuse and repurpose the hotel’s existing furniture to show off their ability to upcycle and demonstrate their styling skills, before they are judged by Michelle and hotel design expert Linda Boronkay.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murders of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: Candyman
programme.desc: For decades, residents of Chicago’s Cabrini-Green have heard stories of a supernatural killer known only as Candyman, allegedly the resurrected soul of a murdered slave wanting to exact vengeance on his killers. In the present day, visual artist Anthony McCoy (Yahya Abdul-Mateen II) and his girlfriend (Teyonah Parris) have moved into a luxury loft condo in a now gentrified Cabrini-Green. A chance encounter with a local old-timer (Colman Domingo) exposes Anthony to the true story behind Candyman and unleashes a wave of violence that puts him on a collision course with destiny.

programme.title: Chaos Walking
programme.desc: 2257. A colony world community without women, where thoughts are heard aloud, is disrupted when teen Todd finds crash survivor Viola, and the pair must flee ruthless Mayor Prentiss.

programme.title: Stacey Dooley
programme.desc: Invited into the traditionally private Gypsy community, Stacey discovers the complex balancing act young women face growing up in one of Britain’s most maligned ethnic minorities.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: Zara McDermott
programme.desc: Zara McDermott heads to the USA and the small town of Moscow, Idaho to find out about the tragic murders of four students and the global online manhunt that followed their deaths.

programme.title: This is BBC Three
programme.desc: Programmes start at 7.00pm

programme.title: The Catch Up
programme.desc: A round-up of the day's news.

programme.title: Asia
programme.desc: Asia has the longest coastline of any continent and the richest coral reefs on earth. Its seas are connected by some of the world’s most powerful currents and are home to over 60 per cent of all marine species. This groundbreaking film features the charismatic creatures of Asia's oceans, never-seen-before spectacles and brand new animal behaviour. From ocean giants in the big blue to tiny fish that climb trees, squid that glow in the dark and grey reef sharks in their hundreds, Asia’s waters are as spectacular as they are diverse.

programme.title: Freddie Flintoff's Field of Dreams
programme.desc: Sporting hero Freddie Flintoff returns to Preston with plans to take his team of unlikely lads on a cricket tour to India. But weeks before departure, a life-changing accident threatens to put a halt to their dreams. One year later, Freddie emerges back to the safety net of his team to announce the trip is back on, but once in India, the team must learn to live with each other and fend for themselves as they prepare to compete in one of the most revered cricketing nations in the world.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: Twenty players enter a grand palace to play the ultimate game of trust and betrayal, hosted by Karan Johar. As they vie for the ten million rupee prize, an early twist sets alliances and rivalries into motion. The Traitors begin their silent sabotage, hiding in plain sight. In the first mission, the players solve riddles aboard a moving train to avert disaster, with their teamwork and quick thinking put to the ultimate test. As night falls, the Traitors step out of the shadows to plot their first move. Clueless of what the night holds, Innocents await the first judgement of their fate in this deadly game.

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: Tensions rise as the players grapple with the aftermath of a shocking murder. The air is heavy with mistrust as the group prepares for the day’s mission - a test of their ability to work together despite growing fractures. In a challenge that pushes them to think like their fellow players, some of them volunteer to spin on a giant wheel, while others try to match answers with them to build the prize pot. At the Circle of Shaq, the Innocents get their first opportunity to strike back and expose the Traitors. Accusations run wild and alliances shift as the group struggles to separate truth from deception. Will the Innocents succeed in identifying the Traitors, or will their web of lies hold strong?

programme.title: The Traitors India
programme.desc: After tasting success in their first attempt, the Innocents feel a renewed sense of hope, but their joy is fleeting as the Traitors ruthlessly claim their next victim in the dead of the night. The players are divided into two groups for a critical mission: collecting gold nuggets by balancing on slippery planks over a water body. The team with the most gold wins a shield, granting one player protection from the next murder, but the mission exposes cracks in alliances as players crack under pressure. After the mission, Karan leaves the players with a shocking twist - a blast from the past - forcing the group to re-evaluate everything they thought they knew.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: Charles is off to a bumpy start, and Olivia is left speechless after meeting her latest match. Meanwhile, Nirvali debriefs with her family, and Dianne gets a pep talk from her mum.

programme.title: Better Date Than Never
programme.desc: A phone call sets Olivia’s heart racing, Charles gets back on the horse, and Nirvali opens up. Meanwhile, 20-year-old Liv already has her wedding planned but needs to find her groom.

programme.title: Matthew Perry and the Ketamine Queen
programme.desc: Documentary investigating events leading up to the shocking death of Friends star Matthew Perry and the woman at the centre of the case, dubbed the 'Ketamine Queen' by US prosecutors. Jasveen Sangha, a drug dealer and dual US-UK citizen, has pleaded guilty to five federal charges in Los Angeles, including one count of distributing ketamine resulting in death or bodily injury. She faces up to 65 years in prison for supplying Perry with the drugs that led to his death. Sangha was one of five people charged in the case. Prosecutors described her home as a 'drug-selling emporium', where authorities found approximately 79 vials of ketamine and other drugs, including methamphetamine, cocaine and Xanax. Presented by Amber Haque, the film features exclusive, first-on-the-record interviews with Sangha's friends and examines her role in Perry’s death and Hollywood’s ketamine drug network.

programme.title: Fake Friend: The Ticket Scammer
programme.desc: How a Somerset high-flyer turned his charm and connections into tools of deception – conning his school friends and hundreds of others while living a life of glamour himself.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Brothers Steve and Stu scramble to put on a last-minute birthday show for Steve’s daughter. If they fail, Steve’s in the doghouse and Stu might end up... dead.

programme.title: BBC Comedy Short Films
programme.desc: Animated sitcom set in 1990s east London, following the lives of the chaotic Potts family. Joyce Potts’s birthday present has been stolen, so her husband Big Jim enlists the help of his fearsome mother-in-law Edna to track it down on the Thames View Estate.
